preview.title: Set up your Bitcoin wallet
preview.content: This wallet runs from your server. You control the funds, so setup starts with access, backups and a few checks before money moves.
preview.setup_content: Save your node and wallet recovery material, then test with a small payment before you rely on this wallet.

welcome.title: Your wallet for everyday Bitcoin
welcome.content: SwissKnife brings Lightning payments, Bitcoin addresses and a reusable Lightning Address into one place, so you can receive, pay and see what needs attention.
welcome.point_one: Receive payments with invoices, addresses or your Lightning Address.
welcome.point_two: Pay from the same wallet when a payment request arrives.
welcome.point_three: See amounts, status and next actions without switching tools.

custody.title: You hold the keys
custody.content: SwissKnife does not custody your bitcoin or keep a spare copy of your secrets. Before you fund the wallet, make sure your backups are real and reachable.
custody.point_one: Choose a strong owner password for this dashboard.
custody.point_two: Keep recovery material somewhere offline that you can find later.
custody.point_three: Test the wallet with a small amount before you add more.

accessibility.title: Keep your server ready
accessibility.content: Lightning works best when your wallet server is online and reachable. A steady setup helps invoices, payments and connected apps work when you need them.
accessibility.point_one: Confirm the server is reachable before receiving real payments.
accessibility.point_two: Keep the server reachable from the apps that use it.
accessibility.point_three: Use test funds first after changing hosting or node settings.

multi_user.title: Decide who can use it
multi_user.content: Start with the owner account, then connect only the people, apps and services that should be able to pay or receive from this wallet.
multi_user.point_one: Give each connected app only the access it needs.
multi_user.point_two: Review access regularly, especially on shared servers.
multi_user.point_three: Add more accounts when your deployment supports them.
